title: UFC Fight NightUFC Fight Night: Adesanya vs. Pyfer

posts.author: Tapology
posts.content: The official Tapology discussion thread for the event!

posts.author: Mantis xX
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Adesanya vs. Pyfer
Israel Adesanya, DEC
49-46, 48-47x2 Uni Dec for a extremely sharp Adesanya, he survives a strong scare and starts taking this fight over as it goes on, transforming former haymakers that land into huge whiff-misses for Pfyer. Great fight.

Grasso vs. Barber II
Maycee Barber, DEC
Split Dec 30-27, 29-28, 28-29. Grasso fights great but Barber seems in control, fresh, and the overall better aggressor of this fight.

Chiesa vs. Price
Niko Price, DEC
Upset Alert!, Price fights a great gameplan, and hurts Chiesa early forcing Michael to fight from behind but also force the engagement. 30-27x2, 29-28 Uni Dec.

Erosa vs. Douglas
Julian Erosa, TKO, R2
Knees and a wild flurry get the finish for Juicy J.

Abdul-Malik vs. Belgaroui
Mansur Abdul-Malik, DEC
Dec 29-28x2, 28-29 Split Dec for MAM.

McKinney vs. Nelson
Terrance McKinney, TKO, R1
Full Mount TKO, McKinney flattens out a turning Nelson and gets the finish.

Bahamondes vs. Musayev
Ignacio Bahamondes, TKO, R2
Spinning back body kick TKO.

Hooper vs. Gibson Jr.
Chase Hooper, SUB, R1
Kneebar submission, Gibson awaits to counter with a gameplan too long whereas Hooper grabs a leg and does not let go.

Tybura vs. Fortune
Tyrell Fortune, TKO, R1
Tybura gets incredibly flustered when hit, Fortune will put it on him and most certainly not stop until Tybura drops to his knees.

O'Neill vs. Fernandes
Casey O'Neill, DEC
30-27, 29-28x2 Uni Dec for a dominant O'Neill.

Stirling vs. Lopes
Navajo Stirling, TKO, R3
Stirling clips an exhausted Lopes in early 3rd, and by mid 3rd this is put away by Strikes on the cage, and the ref sees enough.

Simón vs. Yanez
Adrian Yanez, DEC
Yanez 29-28x2, 29-28 Wild Split Dec.

Thainara vs. Brasil
Alexia Thainara, DEC
30-27x2, 29-28 Uni Dec.

posts.author: Fast_Tulip
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Pyfer by KO - Izzy's defense/chin isn't as good as it used to be. Think he will get caught
Barber by DEC - couple takedowns and it should be an easy win with Grassos bad grappling
Chiesa by DEC - Price is washed, but I think it goes to a decision here due to Chiesa only getting RNC
Douglas by KO - Big KO incoming
Belgaroui by KO - better hands, decent TDD, and a good chin. Peppers MAM with strikes till R3 finish
Nelson by KO - Nelson's got a good chin and can survive till R2 when he takes over
Ba****ndes by DEC - Musayevs is a hard guy to finish, but Ba****ndes should win a DEC here
Hooper by DEC - low level fight, but the more I watch Hoopers striking, the more I was impressed. Don't think he can finish Gibson on the ground here
Fortune by KO - Tybura is washed and it only takes one to put him out
Fernandes by DEC - pretty confident in this one actually, Gabi is pretty good everywhere except her TDs
Sterling by KO - should be able to get a win here
Simon by DEC - lower confidence pick, but his wrestling should win this
Thainara by DEC - can probably just stall all fight to a W

posts.author: Goat******76
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Chiesa vs Price

How in the **** is Niko still getting fights?? Scratch that, how is Alex Morono still getting fights?? Has the average UFC contract extension increased its average from two fights to twelve?? At this rate, every single veteran that’s been around since Obama was in office is going to extend their loss streaks WAY past Sam Alvey’s ****tacular accolade. I’ve seen less red in a Soviet Union marching band.

Anywho, Niko is a weird fighter. Outside of looking like he surgically implanted a coat hanger into his trap muscles, his fighting style is equally as much of a cluster****. He has two finishes via knockout off his back, and is still the only UFC prospect I can think of who’s signature moves on the feet is crescent kicks and standing hammerfists. While he’s rarely boring, his sloppy prodding footwork, terrible composure during strikes and spotty chin have always held him back from being anything more than a flawed but formidable cult fan favorite.

On the flip side, there’s Chiesa, the UFC equivalent of water without ice. Why Chiesa still persists on hanging around the welterweight division in his late 30s, when he likely gets paid equally as much sitting on his ass as a mediocre fight analyst is a life decision that perplexes me. But whatever, I’m not his dad, I just wish I didn’t have to see him hog up space on the main card. Chiesa’s pros and cons as a fighter have been the same and remain the same more than a decade into his tenure: good jiujitsu offense, bad jiujitsu defense, completely **** on the feet. Niko has a small sliver of a chance of winning this fight simply by going full ‘unga bunga’ and swarming the **** out of Chiesa with punches and clinch knees, or simply by Chiesa doing his good ol’ method of shoving his head in places where they don’t belong and getting choked out prematurely in a guillotine or some ****. Personally, I’m gonna take a risk and say Niko inches this one out, more out of sheer stubbornness than actual logical reasoning, and also because I’m crossing my figures this won’t just be fifteen minutes of Chiesa lovingly pressing himself up against Niko’s body heat and whispering sweet nothings into his ear.

Fingers crossed.

Niko via DEC

posts.author: Tazzmann85
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Anyone giving Kyle Nelson a chance over McKinney ?

I actually like Nelson in this one. He’s definitely improved his striking over last few years as well as his cardio, I remember he used to gas really badly and now fights well over the 3 rounds.

We all know what McKinney is going to do, come out balls to the wall and look for the finish, probably the most aggressive fighter in the UFC, usually does not have a plan B if he cannot get the finish in round 1.

I like Nelson to withstand the onslaught of McKinney and get his own finish in round 2 or later in round 1. I can see why majority is on McKinney here but I think this is the best version of Kyle Nelson.

posts.author: Goat******76
posts.content:
[Predictions]

@Tazzmann85  The big word related to this matchup is ‘if’. Personally, as much as I like Nelson, the chances of him surviving the first round are very slim. His grappling is unlikely to save him as McKinney is no slouch on the ground, and his default form of defense is a cage guard, which against the mythical power of a Round 1 McKinney pretty much makes him a sitting duck.

posts.author: connorbrown16
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Alexia Thainara vs. Bruna Brasil
Alexia Thainara via Decision
I’m fading Bruna against any serious fighters in the UFC and Thainara is heading towards the rankings while Bruna goes in the opposite direction. Alexia will mix up some clinch work with ground control and coast to an easy decision victory.

Ricky Simon vs. Adrian Yanez
Ricky Simon via Decision
First time seeing Yanez in over a year so I automatically get suspect about his preparedness for this. This is a hometown fight for Ricky and while he’s more filler than contender now, he’s still pretty game no matter who he’s facing. He should outclass the rusty Yanez who still hasn’t won in about 4 years to a decision win.

Navajo Stirling vs. Bruno Lopes
Navajo Stirling via Decision
You can’t fool me Navajo, I’m too familiar with your game. Even if Bruno loves to get his consciousness taken from him, when has Stirling done that to anyone recently…don’t take the bait. People don’t change; when they show you who they are, believe them. You’re not that guy pal, you’re not that guy. Take your decision victory Navajo and get out of my sight.

Casey O’Neill vs. Gabriella Fernandes
Gabriella Fernandes via Split Decision
The vibes are quickly deteriorating as the decisions mount up on this card. Pretty straightforward here: Section 13: Page 4: Paragraph 3 clearly states and I quote *ahem*…”The Unified Rules of Mixed Martial Arts declares that all known combatants from Fight Ready in Phoenix, Arizona are to be faded on all bets, picks and any other wagers under our United Front”. Rules are rules, Gabby wins by greasy Split.

Marcin Tybura vs. Tyrell Fortune
Marcin Tybura via 2nd Round TKO
There once was a time when I was a young lad that I thought the UFC missed out on Tyrell Fortune. How we all use to think foolish things when we’re young. 40 year old Tybura gives me little to nothing to feel confident in, fug it I bet Forture starts to fade if he doesn’t finish him in the 1st and Tybura pulls a rabbit out of his ass and gets the first finish of the night with a 2nd round knockout.

Chase Hooper vs. Lance Gibson Jr.
Chase Hooper via 1st Round Submission
Now we’re starting to get some action with some finishes. Hooper at home with a pretty favorable matchup; especially in his wheelhouse on the mat, this should be getting the hometown preferential treatment with Hooper locking in the RNC in the 1st and getting the crowd on their feet.

Ignacio Bahamondes vs. Tofiq Musayev
Ignacio Bahamondes via 1st Round TKO
Bahamondes took about a year to get in the lab and work on rebounding after that loss to Fiziev. The size difference and willingness to press forward will be the decider here and I don’t think it’ll take very long. Ignacio will blitz Tofiq and fold him early with authority to get back in the win column.

Terrance McKinney vs. Kyle Nelson
Terrance McKinney via 1st Round KO
It’s Terrance McKinney so it’s automatic a 5 minute fight. I’ve seen Kyle Nelson get the **** knocked out of him my fair share of times and Terrance will clip him early and send him packing.

Mansur Abdul-Malik vs. Yousri Belgaroui
Mansur Abdul-Malik via Decision
Yes, this is a big jump up for Mansur and he’ll have to deal with looks he hasn’t seen but it’s not like he hasn’t planned for how Yousri will attack him on the feet. This is all about pressure and not giving Belgaroui space to operate and Mansur can definitely do that. This is the perfect test for him because he’s going to need to figure out these kinds of strikers but he’ll pass this test with a hard fought decision victory.

Julian Erosa vs. Lerryan Douglas
Lerryan Douglas via 2nd Round KO
Sooner or later, Erosa’s chin will betray him again and Douglas isn’t one to play games with on the feet. He packs some serious heat and will put his lights out if he can’t find a way to control him and no, there’s no pulling guilly here. I see Douglas dropping him with a brutal right hook for a vicious 2nd round stoppage.

Michael Chiesa vs. Niko Price
Michael Chiesa via 1st Round Submission
Mad props to these two on their careers and it’s time to go out with a bang. I give Niko credit for stepping in and the final check will help with his million kids but Chiesa at home in his final fight, he’s going for that finish and he’ll get it with a 1st round RNC to wrap up his MMA career the way he’d want to.

Alexa Grasso vs. Maycee Barber
Maycee Barber via Decision
Winning the belt can be a blessing and a curse sometimes. It made Alexa into a star and losing it started a really rough patch with injuries and inconsistency. I don’t think much of The Headcase after the stunt she pulled when she was supposed to fight Blanchfield but she’s still a viable contender and while I don’t see her winning the belt, she’ll probably be getting a title shot if she doesn’t do anymore dumb ****. If this were Alexa before all the injuries I might take her but not now. The Headcase will put hands and cleavage on her and take the decision win.

Israel Adesanya vs. Joe Pyfer
Israel Adesanya via Decision
If you thought matching Jabroni Pyfer against a washed Adesanya would make me stop fading then “aha” ***** you thought. Izzy isn’t going to let this dude beat him and live to have him remind him about it at every turn. He’s going to outclass him on the feet and ride out a 5 round decision win.
